The Agent Trust Stack.
Skyfire gives agents
verified identity and payment credentials
that work across the open
          internet. No 403 errors, captchas or blocked checkouts. Authenticated access and payment capability,
          everywhere your agents need to be.

What KYA Powers
One token, two surfaces.
An extensible identity token accepted by the majority of the commercial internet, enabling the entire agentic
          customer journey from access to login to checkout.
KYA · Access
Access & Login
A portable, verified identity token accepted by the majority of the commercial internet. KYA is designed to
            give your agent programmatic access and log-in capabilities to websites, applications and agent protocols
            with little engineering effort.

Agentic Commerce
Checkout & Payments
A complete agentic commerce wallet that allows your agents to securely complete checkouts via websites and
            emerging agentic protocols. Skyfire partners with all major credit card networks to allow tokenized card
            transactions and user mandate collection so agents can reliably checkout out at the scale of the internet.
Visa
Mastercard
Discover
USDC
Learn more about Checkout & Payments →
See Use Cases
→
Covering more than
60%
of the Web.
Powering agentic commerce through leading infrastructure partners
f5-logo-black
HUMAN Security
Enable Agent Acceptance
→
Product Stack
One unified trust stack.
01
Know Your
Agent
Give your agents controlled access with a standardized open identity protocol designed to
            unblock websites, services and checkouts.
Learn about KYA
→
02
Agentic
Wallet
Give your agents a secure wallet able to enroll user credit cards, manage stablecoins and
            collect user mandates.
See Use Cases
→
03
Buy
for Me
A complete agentic purchase experience on behalf of users with secure transactions
            leveraging existing referal links for checkout.
See Use Cases
→
Access
Enable Agent Access
at Internet Scale
Teams that operate agents need reliable access to websites and applications. KYA tokens from Skyfire bind
            platform, agent, and human principal identities along with user mandates to allow seamless access at scale,
            across the open web.
Enable Agent Access

Powered by
Developer Documentation
Identity and Payments for AI. This guide provides technical instructions for using the Skyfire platform as both a Buyer and Seller.
Overview
Skyfire enables programmatic interaction between
Buyers
and
Sellers
using a token-based identity and payment protocol.
Each user manages one or more agent accounts (Buyer or Seller).  Buyers interact with seller websites, APIs, MCP servers, and services by presenting Skyfire tokens:
kya
(identity)
pay
(payment)
kya-pay
(combined identity and payment)
Platform Structure
Users
A
User
is the top-level entity on the Skyfire platform. Users typically represent an individual person or business.
Users:
Manage one or more
agent accounts
Maintain a verified identity
Control API keys and wallet access
A user's verified identity may be leveraged by their agents for:
Account creation
Authentication
Access to seller services
Agent Accounts
Agent accounts are the operational entities that interact with Skyfire APIs.
Each agent:
Is tied to exactly one user
Has exactly one role:
Buyer
or
Seller
Is accessed programmatically via unique API keys.
Each user may have:
One
Buyer agent account
(created by default at signup).
One optional
Seller agent account
(created via the Skyfire Dashboard)
Each agent may have one or more API keys for secure access.
Buyer Agent
Acts on behalf of the user to discover and purchase services or tools from sellers.
Capabilities:
Can create accounts with seller services
Subscribe to or purchase services
Generate:
kya
tokens (identity)
pay
tokens (payment)
kya-pay
tokens (identity + payment)
Each buyer agent has a
wallet
, funded by the user, used to transact with sellers.
When calling a seller service, the buyer includes the appropriate token as specified by the seller (e.g. HTTP header or request body).
Seller Agent
Enables a user to publish and monetize services, tools, or APIs.
Seller agents:
Must be explicitly created via the Skyfire Dashboard
Register
Seller Services
Define identity and payment requirements for buyer requests
Verify incoming tokens via standard JWKS libraries to:
Authenticate the buyer (
kya
)
Charge the buyer (
pay
or
kya-pay
)
Sellers receive payments via Skyfire's settlement system once services are delivered.
Wallets
Each agent account comes with an associated wallet, managed by Skyfire.
Buyers
fund their wallets to purchase services and tools
Sellers
receive funds in their wallets after successfully charging buyer tokens and delivering the service
Skyfire handles
settlement and transfer
of funds between wallets after a successful transaction.
Seller Services
Seller services are the products, tools, or APIs made available by seller agents to buyers.
A seller can define
multiple seller services
under one seller agent.
Services may include:
Remote/local MCP servers
APIs
Websites
Each Seller Service specifies:
Pricing
Identity requirements
Accepted token types
Token format and expected delivery method (e.g.
skyfire-pay-id
header)
Identity Requirements
Seller Services may optionally enforce identity requirements.
If
no identity requirements
are specified:
Any buyer can create tokens for it
The buyer principal's email address will be included in the
hid
claim
If identity requirements are specified:
Only buyers that satisfy those identity requirements can create tokens for it
Rules
:
If only
business identity
is required:
Only buyers that have verified their business identity can create tokens
Buyers that have NOT verified identity OR have verified individual identity CANNOT create tokens
If only
individual identity
is required
Only buyers that have verified their individual identity can create tokens
Buyers that have NOT verified identity OR have verified business identity CANNOT create tokens
If both
individual and business identity
is required
Only buyers that have verified either their individual identity or their business identity can create tokens
Buyers that have NOT verified identity at all CANNOT create tokens
Buyers with no verified identity cannot create tokens for restricted services
Services can be:
Paid
, require
pay
or
kya-pay
tokens
Free
, require
kya
tokens (identity only)
Updated 4 months ago
What’s Next
Understanding KYA+Pay Tokens
KYA+Pay Tokens
Did this page help you?
Yes
No

analysis.name: Skyfire
analysis.value_proposition: Identity, access, and payment infrastructure that gives AI agents verified credentials to log in, access, and check out across the open web.
analysis.ideal_customer: Teams building or operating AI agents that need reliable authenticated access, login, and checkout across websites, APIs, and agent protocols.

features.name: know your agent (kya) identity token
features.description: Portable verified identity token binding platform, agent, and human principal identities for agent access.
features.evidence_quote: A portable, verified identity token accepted by the majority of the commercial internet.

features.name: agent access to websites
features.description: Programmatic access to websites without 403s, captchas, or blocked checkouts.
features.evidence_quote: No 403 errors, captchas or blocked checkouts.

features.name: agent login
features.description: Authenticated login linking agent and human identity on target sites.
features.evidence_quote: Site access with KYA, then authenticated login linking agent and human identity.

features.name: agentic wallet
features.description: Secure wallet for agents that enrolls user credit cards, manages stablecoins, and collects user mandates.
features.evidence_quote: Give your agents a secure wallet able to enroll user credit cards, manage stablecoins and collect user mandates.

features.name: tokenized card transactions
features.description: Partnerships with major card networks for tokenized card checkout by agents.
features.evidence_quote: Skyfire partners with all major credit card networks to allow tokenized card transactions.

features.name: stablecoin support
features.description: Wallet supports USDC and stablecoin management for agent transactions.
features.evidence_quote: manage stablecoins and collect user mandates

features.name: user mandate collection
features.description: Collects explicit user mandates for merchant, product, and amount before agent checkout.
features.evidence_quote: User mandate for merchant, product, and amount.

features.name: buy for me
features.description: Complete agentic purchase experience on behalf of users using existing referral links for checkout.
features.evidence_quote: A complete agentic purchase experience on behalf of users with secure transactions leveraging existing referal links for checkout.

features.name: kya token
features.description: Identity-only token used for authenticated agent access to services.
features.evidence_quote: kya (identity)

features.name: pay token
features.description: Payment-only token used to charge a buyer agent for a seller service.
features.evidence_quote: pay (payment)

features.name: kya-pay token
features.description: Combined identity and payment token for a single agent request.
features.evidence_quote: kya-pay (combined identity and payment)

features.name: buyer agent accounts
features.description: Agent identity tied to a user that discovers and purchases services from sellers.
features.evidence_quote: Acts on behalf of the user to discover and purchase services or tools from sellers.

features.name: seller agent accounts
features.description: Agent identity that publishes and monetizes services, tools, or APIs on Skyfire.
features.evidence_quote: Enables a user to publish and monetize services, tools, or APIs.

features.name: api key management
features.description: Each agent may hold one or more API keys for secure programmatic access.
features.evidence_quote: Each agent may have one or more API keys for secure access.

features.name: managed agent wallets
features.description: Skyfire-managed wallets attached to each agent for funding and receiving payments.
features.evidence_quote: Each agent account comes with an associated wallet, managed by Skyfire.

features.name: settlement between wallets
features.description: Skyfire handles settlement and transfer of funds between buyer and seller wallets.
features.evidence_quote: Skyfire handles settlement and transfer of funds between wallets after a successful transaction.

features.name: seller service registration
features.description: Sellers register services (MCP servers, APIs, websites) with pricing and token requirements.
features.evidence_quote: Seller services are the products, tools, or APIs made available by seller agents to buyers.

features.name: mcp server monetization
features.description: Support for monetizing remote or local MCP servers as seller services.
features.evidence_quote: Services may include: Remote/local MCP servers

features.name: jwks token verification
features.description: Sellers verify incoming tokens via standard JWKS libraries.
features.evidence_quote: Verify incoming tokens via standard JWKS libraries

features.name: identity requirements per service
features.description: Sellers can require verified individual and/or business identity to create tokens.
features.evidence_quote: Seller Services may optionally enforce identity requirements.

features.name: individual identity verification
features.description: Buyers can verify individual identity to satisfy seller identity gates.
features.evidence_quote: Only buyers that have verified their individual identity can create tokens

features.name: business identity verification (kyb)
features.description: Buyers can verify business identity to satisfy seller identity gates.
features.evidence_quote: Only buyers that have verified their business identity can create tokens

features.name: skyfire directory
features.description: Directory for discovering seller services and verified service guarantees.
features.evidence_quote: Skyfire Directory / Service Discovery / Verified Service Guarantee

features.name: service discovery
features.description: Buyer agents can discover available seller services via the directory.
features.evidence_quote: Service Discovery

features.name: verified service guarantee
features.description: Guarantee mechanism for verified sellers listed in the Skyfire directory.
features.evidence_quote: Verified Service Guarantee

features.name: buyer onboarding
features.description: Documented onboarding flow for buyer accounts and wallet funding.
features.evidence_quote: Buyer Account Creation / Buyer Onboarding

features.name: seller onboarding
features.description: Documented onboarding flow for seller accounts.
features.evidence_quote: Seller Account Creation / Seller Onboarding

features.name: wallet funding
features.description: Users fund buyer agent wallets to transact with sellers.
features.evidence_quote: Buyers fund their wallets to purchase services and tools

features.name: free service support
features.description: Free seller services accept identity-only kya tokens.
features.evidence_quote: Free, require kya tokens (identity only)

features.name: paid service support
features.description: Paid seller services require pay or kya-pay tokens.
features.evidence_quote: Paid, require pay or kya-pay tokens

features.name: agent protocol support (mcp, ucp, a2a)
features.description: Tokens work across emerging agent protocols including MCP, UCP, and A2A.
features.evidence_quote: Agent Protocols MCP, UCP, A2A…

features.name: human principal binding
features.description: KYA tokens bind platform, agent, and human principal identities together.
features.evidence_quote: KYA tokens from Skyfire bind platform, agent, and human principal identities

features.name: bot management partner integrations
features.description: Integrations with bot management and fraud platforms to enable agent acceptance.
features.evidence_quote: Powering agentic commerce through leading infrastructure partners

features.name: llms.txt endpoint index
features.description: Machine-readable llms.txt index of docs pages and OpenAPI endpoints for AI agents.
features.evidence_quote: visit https://docs.skyfire.xyz/llms.txt for an index of all pages formatted in Markdown and endpoints in OpenAPI.

features.name: kya pay
features.description: KYA Pay product surface listed as a distinct resource.
features.evidence_quote: Resources … KYA Pay

differentiators: Identity token accepted by the majority of the commercial internet (claims >60% web coverage)
Binds platform, agent, and human principal identities in a single token
Combined identity + payment token (kya-pay) for one-shot agent checkout
Works with existing referral links so merchants need no technical lift
Partnerships with all major credit card networks for tokenized agent card transactions
Supports both fiat (Visa/Mastercard/Discover) and stablecoin (USDC) rails

recent_ships.what: Live implementations with Dappier.com, BuildShip.tools, and Apify.com
Smart News Crawler demo — end-user identity for news/content access
Discovery and purchase of premium financial data by an agent
Paying web crawler access to protected/premium web content

pricing.notes: No public pricing page — /pricing returns 404. Buyer wallets are user-funded and sellers receive settlement via Skyfire; per-service pricing is set by sellers, not by Skyfire tiers.

recent_hn.title: Show HN: DialtoneApp Network, card payments for bot commerce
